name: audit-hub-api
description: Single backend service for the Audit Evidence Hub. Owns controls, evidence submissions, evidence images (S3-backed), review decisions, and the audit trail in an embedded SQLite database. Enforces per-role/per-assignment authorization (Compliance Admin sees everything; Team Manager sees only their team's controls; Auditor sees only controls assigned to them for review). Resolves teams, people, and product/system references from the organization's existing directory and asset-registry services rather than storing copies, sends action-required emails via the organization's notification service, and stores/retrieves evidence images in S3. Does not implement sign-in — the platform gateway validates JWTs issued by the shared user-auth dependency.

dependencies.name: audit-hub-webapp
dependencies.description: The SPA this API serves data to; not called by this service, listed for topology completeness.

dependencies.name: user-auth
dependencies.description: Shared end-user auth dependency; the platform derives exposesAPI.auth: end-user-required on this service from it and the gateway injects X-User-Id.

dependencies.name: org-directory-service
dependencies.description: Organization's people/teams directory. Used to resolve teams (for control assignment and team-scoped filtering), managers, and auditors (for reviewer assignment) by reference id — no local copy is kept.

dependencies.name: asset-registry-service
dependencies.description: Organization's product/system registry. Used to resolve the product/system a control is scoped to, by reference id.

dependencies.name: notification-service
dependencies.description: Organization's email/notification service. Used to send action-required emails: to a Team Manager on new assignment or rejection, to an Auditor on new/re-submitted evidence.

dependencies.name: evidence-storage
dependencies.description: S3 bucket storing uploaded evidence images; the API uploads via PutObject on submission and reads back via GetObject/presigned URL on view. Only the object key and metadata are persisted in the database.

componentAgentInstructions: Go service on port 9090 with GET /health (exempt from auth). Use modernc.org/sqlite (driver name "sqlite", DB at /data/audit-hub.db) for controls, evidence submissions, evidence images (S3 object key + metadata only, never bytes), review decisions, and audit-trail rows. No /auth/* endpoints. The API Platform gateway validates the JWT (from the shared user-auth dependency) and injects X-User-Id (from JWT sub claim) on every request; read X-User-Id to identify the caller, reject 401 when missing, and resolve the caller's role/team/auditor assignments via org-directory-service. Do NOT validate JWTs yourself; do NOT add CORS middleware (the gateway handles CORS). Every read/write of controls, evidence, and reviews MUST be filtered per the caller's role: Compliance Admin unrestricted; Team Manager restricted to controls whose teamId matches a team they manage (per org-directory-service); Auditor restricted to controls whose auditorIds include the caller. Upstream org-directory-service, asset-registry-service, notification-service: call via env var <NAME_UPPER_SNAKE>_URL, discover their real contract before coding against them — never guess endpoint paths or payload shapes. Evidence images: use the AWS SDK for Go against evidence-storage (env AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY/AWS_S3_BUCKET_NAME/AWS_REGION), key objects as evidence/<controlId>/<submissionId>/<uuid>-<filename>, store only the key+contentType+size in SQLite. Send emails through notification-service, never a local SMTP client.
